text.title: Browser Automation Anti-patterns
text.summary: Fixed timeouts, inline selectors, concurrency issues, and captcha/anti-bot failure modes.

antipattern.description: Using page.waitForTimeout(2000) or sleep() as the primary wait strategy.
antipattern.reason: Fixed timeouts are either too short (flaky on slow CI) or too long (slow overall); condition-based waits are always correct and faster.

antipattern.description: Generating selectors from imagined HTML without feeding the agent actual DOM or accessibility tree.
antipattern.reason: LLMs invent plausible-looking selectors that don't match the real page; feeding page.content() or the accessibility tree prevents this.

antipattern.description: Spawning N browsers in retry loops without a concurrency cap, causing OOM on the host.
antipattern.reason: Each headless Chromium instance consumes ~50-100MB RAM; uncapped retries on failure exhaust memory within minutes.

antipattern.description: Using CSS class selectors (e.g., .bg-blue-600) in automation scripts on Tailwind-styled sites.
antipattern.reason: Tailwind JIT classes are auto-generated and change on refactors; role= and text= selectors survive styling changes.

antipattern.description: Reusing one browser context across multiple scraping tasks, causing auth state leakage.
antipattern.reason: Cookies and localStorage persist across tasks in a shared context; auth from task A bleeds into task B, causing wrong-user actions.

antipattern.description: Automating payment, account creation, or destructive form submission without a human-in-the-loop confirmation.
antipattern.reason: Irreversible actions (charges, account deletion, data transfer) must be confirmed by a human seeing the rendered form before submission.

rule.statement: Run headed in development (PWDEBUG=1), headless in CI; never debug a headless flake without trace: 'on-first-retry' enabled.
rule.rationale: Headless flakes are invisible without a trace; Playwright Trace Viewer provides timeline, screenshots, and network events to diagnose them.
